prior.content_md:
## The regulation in plain language

The IMO Net-Zero Framework is a proposed addition to MARPOL Annex VI (Prevention of Air Pollution from Ships) as a new Chapter 5. It has two interlocking instruments:

**1. Global Fuel Standard (GFI Standard):** Ships must progressively reduce their annual greenhouse gas fuel intensity — the quantity of GHG emitted per unit of energy used — calculated on a well-to-wake basis. Two compliance tiers exist: a Base Target and a Direct Compliance Target. Ships meeting or exceeding the Direct Compliance Target earn surplus units.

**2. Global Economic Measure (GHG Pricing Mechanism):** Ships emitting above GFI thresholds must acquire "remedial units" to balance their emissions deficit. Remedial units are purchased through contributions to the IMO Net-Zero Fund. Ships at or above the Direct Compliance Target earn financial rewards from that Fund. The mechanism is designed to create a price signal for clean fuel adoption across the global fleet.

**IMO Net-Zero Fund:** Revenues from remedial unit contributions will be disbursed to reward low-emission ships, fund innovation and infrastructure in developing countries, and mitigate impacts on Small Island Developing States and Least Developed Countries.

**Scope:** Mandatory for large ocean-going ships over 5,000 gross tonnage, which collectively emit approximately 85% of total CO₂ from international shipping.

**Objective:** Net-zero GHG emissions from international shipping by or around 2050, consistent with the 2023 IMO GHG Strategy (Resolution MEPC.377(80)).

*Source: IMO Press Briefing, "IMO approves net-zero regulations for global shipping," IMO, 11 April 2025. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/pressbriefings/pages/imo-approves-netzero-regulations.aspx. Resolution MEPC.377(80), IMO, 7 July 2023. https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/KnowledgeCentre/IndexofIMOResolutions/MEPCDocuments/MEPC.377(80).pdf. MARPOL overview: https://www.imo.org/en/about/conventions/pages/international-convention-for-the-prevention-of-pollution-from-ships-(marpol).aspx.*

## Why it applies to the workspace

The workspace operates as a freight forwarder across Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lanes. Ocean freight is tertiary by mode volume but is actively used. The workspace does not operate vessels and therefore bears no direct compliance obligation under the GFI Standard or pricing mechanism — those obligations fall on vessel operators and carriers. However, the workspace is exposed through two indirect channels:

- **Cost pass-through:** Carriers facing remedial unit costs will incorporate those costs into ocean freight rates, either as fuel surcharge line items or embedded in base rates. Every ocean shipment the workspace books will carry this cost layer once the mechanism is in force. The workspace has no ability to avoid or offset this cost on the carrier side.
- **Client Scope 3 reporting:** Clients in the live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian verticals that are subject to sustainability disclosure requirements (CSRD, ISSB, or equivalent) will need GHG intensity data for ocean freight legs. The GFI Standard's well-to-wake calculation methodology will affect how emissions are reported and attributed for Scope 3 purposes.

*Source: IMO Press Briefing, IMO, 11 April 2025 (as above). Compliance obligation structure: MARPOL Annex VI framework, https://www.imo.org/en/ourwork/environment/pages/air-pollution.aspx.*
